verdict_reason: The single disagreement (klaibyam) is well-explained. It identifies the term, names the literal etymology (state of a klība — a eunuch), names the modern translation choices (preserve the gendered force vs. render as faintheartedness/cowardice), and identifies the doctrinal/translation axis at stake (cultural register choice — Krishna's shame-tactic depends partly on the gendered force; modern translations choose to soften this). One tight line, doctrinally substantive, no winner picked. Gold-standard quality.
